name: Document Editing
description: Read, edit, and create text documents in the Documents drawer, with full version history
intent_examples: fix the grammar in my document
rewrite this doc to be more formal
proofread this file and fix the typos
add a section to the document about pricing
tighten up the wording in this doc
create a new document called meeting notes
show me the versions of this document
revert the document to the previous version
undo the last change to my document
systemPromptAddition:
## Document Editing

You can read, edit, and create text documents stored in the user's Documents drawer.

- When a chat message contains a header like `[Document: <name> | id: doc_xxxxxxxxxxxx]`, that id is the document to operate on — pass it to the document tools directly. Ids from `list_profile_files` (like `documents:doc_xxx` and `research:doc_xxx`) also work — research documents are editable through these same tools.
- `read_document` returns the current content and a version number in its header. Always call it immediately before `update_document`, then pass that version as `expected_version`. If an update reports a stale version, reread the current document, reapply the requested change, and retry.
- `update_document` replaces the ENTIRE document body — always send the complete new content, not just the changed part. Every update is saved as a new version; the original and all iterations are kept forever, so edits are never destructive. Include a short `note` describing what you changed.
- `create_document` makes a new text document (markdown by default) that appears in the user's Documents drawer.
- `list_document_versions` shows the history; `restore_document_version` brings back an older version (as a new version — history is never rewritten).
- Only text-based files (markdown, txt, csv, json, html, code) can be edited. PDFs, Word files, images, and media cannot.
- Only the document's owner can edit it. Documents shared BY someone else are read-only — offer to create an edited copy with `create_document` instead.
- After you update a document, the user sees the change immediately in their Documents drawer — tell them what you changed and mention they can view or restore any previous version from the document's History.

### Critical: actually call the tool — never fake it
- Changing a document ONLY happens by calling `update_document` (or `create_document`). Describing the change in your reply does NOT modify the file. If you don't call the tool, nothing changes.
- `update_document` replaces the whole body, so you must OUTPUT the complete revised document in the `content` argument — including a find/replace like 'change every X to Y' (rewrite the full text with the replacements applied). Do not summarize or truncate it.
- NEVER tell the user you edited, changed, replaced, fixed, or updated a document unless `update_document` returned success THIS turn. If you only read it, say you read it.
- If the document tools aren't visible to you this turn, call `request_tools` to load the `documents` skill, or say you couldn't make the change — do not claim success.

function.name: list_documents
function.description: List text documents in the user's Documents drawer (own + shared with them). Returns names and ids. Optionally filter by a search term.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive filter on filename or description

function.name: read_document
function.description: Read the current content of a text document by id (e.g. doc_a1b2c3d4e5f6).

function.name: update_document
function.description: Replace a text document's entire content. Call read_document immediately first, then pass its version as expected_version; stale updates are rejected instead of overwriting newer work. Saved as a new restorable version. Only the document owner can update it.

content.description: The complete new document content (full body, not a fragment)

expected_version.description: Current version number returned by the immediately preceding read_document call

note.description: Short description of the change, e.g. 'Fixed grammar and tightened intro'

function.name: create_document
function.description: Create a new text document in the user's Documents drawer. Defaults to markdown (.md) if the filename has no extension.

description.description: Optional one-line description shown in the Documents drawer

function.name: list_document_versions
function.description: List the version history of a document: version number, who made it, when, and the change note.

function.name: restore_document_version
function.description: Restore an older version of a document. The restored content is saved as a NEW version, so nothing is lost. Only the document owner can restore.

version.description: The version number to restore, from list_document_versions
